在Axios发布的这封措辞严厉的信中，拜登竞选经理简·奥马利·狄龙(Jen O‘Malley Dillon)特别提到了特朗普竞选团队上周在Facebook和Twitter上分享的一段令人不安的视频。
在这段视频的过程中，总统的儿子声称，他父亲的政治对手“计划增加数百万张可以取消你的投票并推翻选举的欺诈性选票”，并呼吁支持者“现在就征募”参加“特朗普军队选举安全行动”。这些虚假声明似乎激励了特朗普的一些支持者，他们计划守卫选票投放点和投票站-这是一种选民恐吓的形式，很可能构成联邦犯罪。
当拜登竞选团队(以及其他许多人)将这段视频标记给Facebook时，该公司显然表示，这些内容不会被删除，并指出其小小的、不引人注目的投票信息标签出现在所有与2020年美国大选相关的帖子旁边。这段视频仍然在推特上贴着类似的标签。
奥马利·狄龙在写给马克·扎克伯格的信中写道：“我们得到保证，贴在视频上的标签埋在屏幕的右上角，许多观众会错过它，应该会减轻任何担忧，”奥马利·狄龙在写给马克·扎克伯格的信中写道。
“没有一家公司认为自己是民主中一股有益的力量，并声称认真对待选民压制，不会允许这种危险的哗众取宠传播给数百万人。根据您的政策，删除此视频本应是最简单的要求，但它至今仍在使用。“。
在信中，奥马利·狄龙还引用了总统自己多次试图用未经证实的关于投票过程的谎言来破坏国家对2020年选举的信心，今年投票过程已经面临着独特的压力。
Facebook没有采取强有力的方法来限制来自总统和他的支持者的与选举相关的虚假信息的影响，而是在很大程度上保持了不插手。该平台更愿意兜售其退出投票活动和其他政治中立的努力，以告知和动员选民。Facebook显然希望这些措施能抵消其目前传播来自总统本人的国内虚假信息的作用，但考虑到正在发生的事情的范围-以及自2016年以来挥之不去的失败-这看起来不太可能。
“正如你所说，‘投票就是声音。’Facebook承诺不会让这一声音被一场虚假信息风暴淹没，但未能抓住每一个机会兑现这一承诺，“奥马利·狄龙写道，并补充说，拜登竞选团队将在大选前的剩余36天内”呼喊这些失败“。
note_en:
In the scathing letter, published by  Axios, Biden Campaign Manager Jen O’Malley Dillon specifically singled out a troubling video post the Trump campaign shared to Facebook and Twitter last week.
 Over the course of that video, the president’s son claims that his father’s political opponents “plan to add millions of fraudulent ballots that can cancel your vote and overturn the election” and calls on supporters to “enlist now” in an “Army for Trump election security operation.” Those false claims appear to have inspired some Trump supporters, who plan to  guard ballot drop-off sites and polling places — a form of voter intimidation that would likely constitute a federal crime.
   When the Biden campaign (along with many others) flagged the video to  Facebook,   the company apparently said that the content would not be removed, pointing to its small, unobtrusive voting info labels that appear alongside all posts related to the 2020 U.S. election. The video remains up on Twitter with a similar label.
 “We were assured that the label affixed to the video, buried on the top right corner of the screen were many viewers will miss it, should allay any concerns,” O’Malley Dillon wrote in the letter, addressed to Mark Zuckerberg.
 “No company that considers itself a force for good in democracy, and that purports to take voter suppression seriously, would allow this dangerous claptrap to be spread to millions of people. Removing this video should have been the easiest of easy calls under your policies, yet it remains up today.”
  In the letter, O’Malley Dillon also cites the president’s own repeated attempts to undermine national confidence in the 2020 election with unsubstantiated lies about the voting process, which is already under unique strain this year from the pandemic.
 Rather than taking a strong approach to limit the reach of election-related disinformation from the president and his supporters, Facebook has largely remained hands-off. The platform is more comfortable touting its  get out the vote campaign and  other politically neutral efforts to inform and mobilize voters. Facebook clearly hopes those measures will offset its current role disseminating domestic disinformation from the president himself, but given the scope of what’s happening — and its lingering failures from 2016 — that doesn’t look likely.
 “As you say, ‘voting is voice.’ Facebook has committed to not allow that voice to be drowned out by a storm of disinformation, but has failed at every opportunity to follow through on that commitment,” O’Malley Dillon wrote, adding that the Biden campaign would “be calling out those failures” over the course of the remaining 36 days until the election.
title: 敲响警钟，拜登竞选团队称Facebook是投票虚假信息的“首要传播者”
title_en: Ringing alarm bells, Biden campaign calls Facebook ‘foremost propagator’ of voting disinformation

MLT创始人兼首席执行官约翰·赖斯告诉TechCrunch：“我们的观点是，这必须是一个全面的方法。”“这不仅仅是一个编码计划、导师计划、奖学金计划。有很多很棒的。他们很重要。但我们要说的是，你必须在所有这些杠杆上下功夫，并且要有长远的眼光。我们的观点是，我们真的可以成倍增长，在科技生态系统中杠杆率最高的领域增加少数人的参与度。“。
首先，这种多方面的伙伴关系将使Greylock能够利用MLT由大约8000名黑人、拉丁族和土著专业人员组成的网络，并将他们与该公司投资组合公司的潜在角色联系起来。此外，Greylock和MLT将合作支持留住这些公司，并帮助MLT专业人员从事风险投资职业。
Greylock合伙人David Sze告诉TechCrunch：“在Greylock，看到过去20年的科技生态系统，很明显，现代技术是创造财富的最大机会之一，这对我们来说并不令人意外。”“一直是最伟大的财富创造者之一，在未来--在可预见的未来，很可能也是如此。”
但最大的财务回报是创始人、早期员工和投资者。这就创造了这个网络，Facebook或谷歌等顶级公司的早期员工和校友随后会成为财富创造周期中下一代初创企业的创始人，Sze说。
他说，然后，风投们急于支持那些曾经在这些高增长公司工作的人组成的团队。
“这就是硅谷的运作方式，”史说。“这本身就是一个社交网络。[…]。但问题是，黑人、拉丁裔和美国原住民实际上在很大程度上被排除在科技初创公司、风险投资和这些网络之外。因此，它实际上是一个复合因素。“。
Sze说，对于系统中的那些人来说，这对他们有利，但这意味着对于那些被排除在外的人来说，更难弄清楚如何闯入它。
“看，风投和科技初创公司--我们必须坦诚地说，我们在做对这件事上做得非常糟糕，”施正荣说。“从历史上看，我的意思是，在多样性和包容性方面，我们让系统在没有太多自上而下监督的情况下发展，我们真的需要改变这一点。”
这就是Greylock和MLT合作的一个关键原因，他们试图在这些科技初创公司中吸引更多的黑人、拉丁裔和原住民。而且这并不是说存在管道问题，因为有足够的可用人才，Sze说。但他表示，如果出现管道问题，“问题实际上在我们这边.”
“这不是在人才方面，”史说。“这里有很多人才。这是因为硅谷存在并随着时间的推移而发展起来的网络和系统一直不利于将这一群体纳入其中。“。
Greylock的合作伙伴还捐赠了500万美元，以锚定MLT有史以来第一只Impact基金，这使得MLT能够成为Greylock最新基金的有限合伙人，这是一只10亿美元的基金。
“我们的有限合伙人有着悠久的历史，”施说。“我们不会经常让新的有限合伙人进入，我们对他们的参与感到超级兴奋，因为我们认为这是一种力量倍增。”
Sze说，这种合作伙伴关系的希望是，它将刺激与风险投资基金进行其他合作的想法。对于赖斯来说，他希望其他科技领袖能注意到这一点，并加入到推动这一进程的行列中来。
赖斯说：“领导人需要在这个时候，在这个关键时刻，更好地了解我们为什么会出现这种情况。”“[…]。领导者不仅需要消息灵通，而且愿意承担责任，以获得更多的信息。这并不要求他们成为种族主义历史方面的专家。这需要他们理解，就像他们理解人工智能和比特币之类的东西一样。了解这些东西。“。
赖斯说，领导层看起来也像是承诺采取全面的方法，其严谨程度与风险资本家对公司投资的方式相同，科技公司也适用于公司的增长。
“如果我们在方法上没有同样的严谨性，我们只是认为我们可以通过随机的多样化行为来推动针的移动，那么我们就完蛋了。”我们不会移动针头的。这将需要，你知道，一个全面的方法。“
note_en:
“Our view is this has to be a comprehensive approach,” MLT Founder and CEO John Rice told TechCrunch. “This is not just a coding program, mentor program, fellowship program. There are plenty of great ones. They’re important. But what we’re saying is you have to work on all these levers and take a long-term view. Our view is we can really move the needle exponentially to grow minority participation in the highest leverage areas of the tech ecosystem.”
 For starters, the multi-faceted partnership will enable Greylock to tap into MLT’s network of around 8,000 Black, Latinx and Indigenous professionals and connect them with potential roles at the firm’s portfolio companies. Additionally, Greylock and MLT will work together to support retention at those companies, as well as help MLT professionals pursue careers in venture capital.
  “Being at Greylock and seeing the tech ecosystem over the last 20 years — it’s become pretty clear that, at no surprise to us, modern technology is one of the greatest opportunities for wealth creation,” Greylock Partner David Sze told TechCrunch. “Has been one of the greatest creators for wealth and is likely to be so in the future — in the foreseeable future.”
 But the greatest financial returns accrue to founders, early employees and investors. That creates this network where those early employees and alumni from top companies like Facebook or Google then go on to become founders of the next generation of startups in the wealth creation cycle, Sze said.
  Then, VCs are eager to back teams with people who used to work at those high-growth companies, he said.
 “That’s just how the Valley works,” Sze said. “It’s a social network in and of itself. […] But the issue is that Black and Latinx and Native American people really largely have been left out of tech startups and venture capital and those networks. And as a result, it actually is a compounding factor.”
 For those folks in the system, it compounds in their favor but that means for those left out, it becomes harder to figure out how to break into it, Sze said.
 “And look, VCs and tech startups — we just have to be honest that we’ve been really bad at getting this right,” Sze said. “Historically, I mean, we’ve let the system sort of evolve without much top down oversight in regards of diversity and inclusion and we just really need to change that.”
 That’s a key reason why Greylock and MLT are partnering to try to get more Black, Latinx and Indigenous people in these tech startups. And it’s not that there is a pipeline problem because there is plenty of available talent, Sze said. But he said that if there is a pipeline problem, “the problem is actually on our side.”
 “It’s not on the talent side,” Sze said. “There is plenty of talent out there. It’s that the networks and systems that have existed and grown over time in the valley have not been conducive to allowing the inclusion of that group.”
 Greylock’s partners also donated $5 million to anchor MLT’s first-ever impact fund, which allows MLT to be a limited partner in  Greylock’s latest fund, a $1 billion fund.
 “We have a long history with our LPs,” Sze said. “We do not let new LPs in very often and we’re super excited to have them involved because we think it’s a force multiplier.”
 The hope with this partnership is that it’ll spur ideas for other collaborations with VC funds, Sze said. For Rice, he hopes that other leaders in tech will take note and get on board with moving the needle.
 “Leaders need to be at this time, at this critical juncture, be much better informed about why we are where we are,” Rice said. “[…] Leaders not only need to be well-informed but also be willing to hold themselves accountable to be more informed. And that doesn’t require them to be experts on the history of racism. It requires them to understand like they understand, you know, AI and bitcoin and things like that. Understand this stuff.”
 Leadership, Rice said, also looks like committing to a comprehensive approach with the same level of rigor that venture capitalists apply to how they invest in companies, and that tech companies apply to their growth.
 “If we don’t have that same level of rigor in our approach and we just think that we can move the needle with random acts of diversity, then we’re done. We’re not going to move the needle. It’s going to require, you know, a comprehensive approach.”
title: Greylock和MLT正试图使科技公司的财富周期多样化
title_en: Greylock and MLT are trying to diversify tech’s wealth cycle

伯德·布利奇是Patientco的首席执行官，Patientco是一家专门为重新思考医疗支付体验而成立的支付技术公司。
随着病毒继续给该行业带来新的挑战，投资者正争先恐后地向涉及从远程医疗到患者金融参与等医疗保健子行业的初创企业注入资金。
美国医疗体系的低效和挫折感使其成为以颠覆性为导向的风投们诱人的目标。但这里有一个残酷的事实：医疗保健不同于任何其他行业。从长远来看，它有一堆“快速行动”的初创公司无法应对的监管规定，这是一家“急转直下”的初创公司无法应对的。
医疗保健也是一个敏感的个人问题。因此，患者天生不愿适应新技术，即使他们对现状不满意。因此，至关重要的是，这一领域的初创技术领导者明白如何涉水度过这些不可预测的水域，以便蓬勃发展，为投资者带来强劲的投资回报。
风投们看到了所有关于新冠肺炎的最新头条，他们发现了一个潜在的赚钱机会，将资本投资于创新的初创企业。但是，在提供以患者为中心、以技术为中心的解决方案时，他们必须克服进入壁垒，才能与传统参与者竞争。俗话说得好，“运气就是准备遇到机遇。”在医疗保健创业领域，新冠肺炎为那些在形势演变成危机之前就准备好为市场提供解决方案的人提供了一个机会。在医疗保健创业领域，新冠肺炎为那些在形势演变为危机之前准备好为市场提供解决方案的人提供了一个机会。
因此，风投和私募股权投资者应该把重点放在潜在初创公司试图解决的问题上，因为最近的时代迅速重塑了对某些解决方案的需求。有没有其他主导市场的关键参与者，或者这家初创公司是目前可用的重复产品吗？如果价值主张是独一无二的，它可能会很有趣。如果不是，投资者可能要三思而后行。
note_en:
Bird Blitch is the CEO of  Patientco, a payment technology company founded specifically to rethink the healthcare payment experience.
  As the virus continues to pose new challenges for the industry, investors are rushing to pump money into startups addressing healthcare sub-sectors ranging from telemedicine to patient financial engagement.
 The inefficiencies and frustrations of the U.S. healthcare system make it a tempting target for disruption-oriented VCs. But here’s the hard truth: Healthcare is unlike any other industry. It has a morass of regulations that a “move-fast-and-break-things” startup can’t handle over the long term.
 Healthcare is also a sensitive, personal issue. As such, patients are inherently reluctant to adapt to new technologies, even when they’re dissatisfied with the status quo. Consequently, it’s crucial that startup technology leaders in this space understand how to wade through these unpredictable waters in order to thrive and deliver a strong ROI for investors.
   VCs are seeing all the latest headlines about COVID-19 and spying a potential money-making opportunity to invest capital into innovative startups. However, they must overcome barriers to entry when offering patient-focused, technology-centric solutions before they can compete with legacy players. As the saying goes, “Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity,” and, within the healthcare startup space, COVID-19 presents an opportunity for those who stood ready to offer a solution to the market before the situation became a crisis.
 Therefore, VC and PE investors should focus on the problem the potential startup is trying to solve as recent times have rapidly refashioned the need for certain solutions. Are there other key players leading the market, or is the startup a duplicative offering that is currently available? If the value proposition is unique, it may be interesting. If it’s not, investors may want to think twice.
title: 医疗保健企业家应该为即将到来的风险投资/私募股权泡沫做好准备
title_en: Healthcare entrepreneurs should prepare for an upcoming VC/PE bubble

note:
达拉斯Shipt购物者、Gig Workers Collective的首席组织者威利·索利斯(Willy Solis)在接受TechCrunch采访时表示：“我们的目标是让人们注意到，这个工资标准确实影响了购物者，无论Shipt的立场是什么，考虑到努力和造福购物者，我们发现这两条战线都是相反的，”威利·索利斯(Willy Solis)是达拉斯的Shipt购物者，也是Gig Workers Collective的首席组织者。“这不符合真实的现实。我们付出的努力越多，得到的报酬就越少。“。
Shipt购物者还计划于10月19日(周一)在Target位于明尼苏达州明尼阿波利斯的公司总部举行直接行动。在行动期间，购物者计划阅读写给Shipt首席执行官凯利·卡鲁索的信，信中描述了薪酬变化对他们的影响。
Shipt发言人莫莉·斯奈德(Molly Snyder)在一份声明中告诉TechCrunch：“我们已经向购物者传达了我们在开展业务的六年中学到了很多东西。”“我们以前的支付模式是佣金模式，根据篮子或订单的成本支付。我们知道在购物和送货上付出了多少努力，并相信我们应该补偿这一努力。新的模型通过考虑订单复杂性、所处的市场以及一周中的日期和时间等因素来解释这一努力。购物者总是会看到商店的支付范围，他们需要送货的地址，商店里所有商品的清单和送货窗口的时间框架。购物者可以选择是否接受订单。“。
今年早些时候，在Shipt开始测试这种新的薪酬结构后，Shipt的购物者一直在公开反对这种新的薪酬模式。今年2月，卡拉马祖的一位Shipt购物者告诉我，由于这一变化，他们损失了大约30%或更多的正常工资。
根据Target旗下的Shipt，它这样做是为了“更好地说明完成和交付订单所需的实际努力，”Shipt在Shipt Shopper Hub中写道。这意味着新的支付模式考虑了从商店到顾客门口的预计驾车时间，订单中有多少商品，位置，购物高峰期等等。但Shipt并没有分享确切的薪酬计算公式，因为“每个地铁都有独特的特征，可以影响购物体验。”
在博客上，Shipt还指出，由于付出了努力，价格相似的订单可能会支付不同的费用。例如，如果订单总额为100美元，但只有1个项目而不是30个项目，则后一种订单方案需要付出更多努力。这意味着购物者会从商品越多的订单中获得更多的报酬。但索利斯表示，这是一种反常现象，大多数购物者不会收到这样的订单。
他说：“把整个薪酬结构建立在这样一个反常的基础上真的很令人担忧。”
与此同时，索利斯表示，他发现Shipt谈论薪酬计算公式的方式存在差异。今年7月，Shipt发表了一篇关于商店时间的博客文章。在这封信中，该公司阐述了它是如何考虑物品位置、商店规模等问题的。在后来更新的原始帖子中，Shipt表示没有考虑结账时间，也不是试图了解这一点。
在购物者在Facebook的一个群中表达了对此的失望之后，Solis注意到Shipt从其博客帖子中删除了这一部分。
索利斯说：“他们直截了当地说，他们对考虑结账时间不感兴趣，而结账时间是购物者在商店里花费的相当长的时间。”
Shipt购物者以前也采取过行动，但索利斯表示，这一次得到了迄今为止最多的支持。作为行动号召的一部分，GIG Workers Collective还要求Shipt购物者将这一消息传播给至少其他五名他们认识的工人。
斯奈德说：“我们正在继续听取购物者的反馈，但我们可以告诉你，我们一直在看到越来越多的购物者将自己列入购物、接受、购物和送货的时间表。”
note_en:
“Our goal is to draw attention to the fact that this pay scale really does affect shoppers and regardless of Shipt’s position of it taking into account effort and benefitting shoppers, we are finding it is the opposite on both fronts,” Willy Solis, a Shipt shopper in Dallas and lead organizer at Gig Workers Collective, told TechCrunch. “It’s not holding up to the true reality. We are getting paid less for more effort.”
 Shipt shoppers also plan to stage a direct action at Target’s corporate headquarters in Minneapolis, Minnesota on Monday, October 19. During the action, shoppers plan to read letters written to Shipt CEO Kelly Caruso that describe how the pay changes have impacted them.
 “We have communicated to shoppers that we have learned a lot in the six years we have been in business,” Shipt spokesperson Molly Snyder told TechCrunch in a statement. “Our previous pay model was a commission model that paid based on the cost of the basket or the order. We know how much effort goes into shopping and delivering and believe that we should compensate for that effort. The new model accounts for that effort by factoring in things like the order complexity, what market it is in and the day and time of week. Shoppers will always see the pay range for the shop, the address where they would need to deliver it, a list of all of the items in the shop and the delivery window timeframe. Shoppers can choose whether or not they want to accept an order.”
 Shipt shoppers  have been speaking out against this new pay model since earlier this year, after Shipt started testing this new pay structure.  In February, a Shipt shopper from Kalamazoo told me they were losing about 30% or more of their regular pay as a result of the change.
 According to Target-owned Shipt, it’s doing this to “better account for the actual effort it takes to complete and deliver orders,”  Shipt wrote in the Shipt Shopper Hub. That means the new pay model takes into account estimated drive time from the store to the customer’s door, how many items are in the order, location, peak shopping windows and more. But Shipt isn’t sharing an exact formula for calculating pay because “ each metro has unique characteristics that can affect the shopping experience.”
 On the blog, Shipt also points to how similarly priced orders might pay differently as a result of the effort it takes. For example, if the order total is $100 but is only one item versus 30 items, the latter order scenario would take more effort. That means the shopper would get paid more for that order with more items. But Solis said that’s an anomaly and that the majority of shoppers don’t receive orders like that.
 “To base an entire pay structure off of an anomaly like that is really concerning,” he said.
 Meanwhile, Solis said he’s found discrepancies between the way Shipt talks about its formula for calculating pay. In July,  Shipt published a blog post about shop time. In it, the company laid out how it thinks about things like the location of the item, size of store and more. In the original post,  which has since been updated, Shipt said it did not take into account checkout time, nor was it trying to gain insight about it.
  After shoppers expressed frustration about it in a Facebook group, Solis noticed that Shipt deleted that part from its blog post.
  “They literally said they are not interested in taking into account checkout times, which is a considerable amount of time shoppers spend in stores,” Solis said.
 Shipt shoppers have staged actions before, but Solis said this one is receiving the most support to date. As part of the call-to-action, Gig Workers Collective is also asking Shipt shoppers to spread the word to at least five other workers they know.
 “We are continuing to listen to shopper feedback, but can tell you that we are consistently seeing increasing numbers of shoppers putting themselves on the schedule to shop, accepting, shopping and delivering orders,” Snyder said.
title: 船运购物者正在组织罢工，抗议新的支付模式
title_en: Shipt shoppers are organizing a walkout in protest of new pay model

note:
事实上，正好在一年前，自由职业者联盟和一个面向自由职业者的数字平台Upwork发布了一份报告，估计35%的美国劳动力已经开始从事自由职业者。由于新冠肺炎仍在全国和全球各地奔波，促使数千万人大规模且持续地失业，这一比例可能会迅速上升。
毫不奇怪，精明的初创公司看到了这些人的经济力量-他们中的许多人除了自己业务的稳步增长外，对管理任何人或任何事情都不感兴趣。Collective就是一个很好的例子，这是一家成立2.5年、拥有20名员工的旧金山初创公司，它一直在悄悄地为其所谓的“一个人的企业”所有者提供税务准备和簿记等后台服务，刚刚完成了865万美元的种子融资。
General Catalyst和QED投资者与一系列知名天使投资者共同牵头了这轮融资，其中包括优步联合创始人加勒特·坎普(Garrett Camp)、Figma创始人迪伦·菲尔德(Dylan Field)和DoorDash高管戈库尔·拉贾拉姆(Gokul Rajaram)。
昨天，我们采访了联合创始人兼首席执行官胡曼·拉德法尔，谈到了Collective“赋予、支持和连接个体户社区”的使命--以及它到底提出了什么建议。
TC：你之前成立了一家公司，甚至在2016年甲骨文出售给甲骨文之前，你就跳槽到了风险投资公司，在加勒特·坎普的初创工作室Expa与他合作。为什么要转回创建者模式呢？
HR：在AddThis和Expa以及我的天使投资中，我从头到尾看到的是，管理财务是很困难的。会计、税务、合规--所有这些小企业的设置都很烦人。
两年前，[Collective Co创始人]尤格·卡纳(Uger[Kaner])来到Expa，他基本上向我推销了一个箱式初创公司(Startup-in-a-box)项目，我们从孵化的角度讨论这个项目，但[更有针对性地关注后台问题]。他和我一样是移民，因为他不太了解这个系统，他最终受到了税收处罚-当你是自由职业者的时候，处罚甚至更严重。一些初创企业已经提出了我们提供的定制版本，但我们当时的反应是，他们为什么要这么做？这些都是商品，但如果你把它们放在一个平台上，它们就可以变得强大起来。
TC：那么您所创建的是专有的，还是您正在与第三方合作？
HR：两个都有。我们是一家在线礼宾公司，专注于以后台为核心，即会计和税务服务。我们还为你组建了一家S公司，因为你可以节省很多钱(与以有限责任公司的形式成立企业相比，后者的特点是不同的税收要求)。因此，上面有一个集成层和一个仪表板。如果你是S公司，你需要有工资单，所以我们与Gusto有合作伙伴关系，这是随你的订阅一起提供的。我们与Quickbook有合作关系。我们在合规方面与第三方合作。我们的愿景是让这件事对你来说变得容易，并将其设置为自动驾驶，因为我们明白，时间就是金钱。
对于税收、会计、商业银行和工资，核心方案是每月200美元。我们正在试点簿记和更全面的一揽子服务，这可能是我们随着时间的推移而发展的方向，这将是额外的费用。
TC：你怎么才能说服这些商家相信那笔费用是值得的呢？
HR：在美国，有近300万人(只雇佣自己)年收入超过10万美元，如果你想想他们已经在使用这些(不同的产品)中有多少，这是一笔很大的交易。QuickBooks和Gusto在我们这里更便宜。你可以通过消费来节省开支。魔力真的是以正确的方式运行你的S公司。其中一部分是正常的所得税，但你也有分配，而且它的征税方式与收入不同-它的征税较少。因此，我们调出薪资数据，看看支出情况和各州的情况，然后说，‘这是我们根据你的现金流收入情况向你推荐的，这样你就能以一种合规的方式认识到这种分布。’
TC：对您将从客户那里收集的有用数据很感兴趣。你会怎么使用它呢？
HR：我们首先关心的是确保合适的人看到它(这意味着我们关注的是隐私)。但是，一旦我们获得了使用这些数据的权利，我们就可以在这些数据的聚合方面做很多事情。在我们可以做的事情中，理论上，包括创造一个新的得分水平。例如，如果你是一家只有一家公司的企业，很难获得抵押贷款，因为信用机构没有评估你的工具。但是如果我们有你多年的财务记录，我们能代表你是一个伟大的人吗？你的生意很棒。
随着我们接触到更多的会员-我们很快就会达到2000人-另一个有趣的方向是利用我们作为集体的力量为我们的会员提供更便宜的保险，[帮助促进]信贷，[帮助他们]401(K)计划。
TC：从项目管理到平面设计，大概还有很多其他的东西你也可以涉足。。。
HR：现在，我们想要确保我们的核心服务是明确的。
想想优步给拼车带来的透明度和安心，或者优步Eats给送餐带来的透明度和安心。你知道什么东西什么时候在煮，什么时候在路上，什么时候到。我们已经习惯了这么多事情的透明度和问责性，但当涉及到会计时，它就不在那里了，这太疯狂了。我们想要改变这一点。
TC：追求“一家企业”意味着你面对的是一个高度分散的市场。为了接触到潜在客户，你们想要建立什么样的合作伙伴关系？
HR：我们现在正在进行这些对话，但你可以想象新银行以及护士、医生、房地产经纪人和作家的垂直市场是有意义的。有很多可能性。
图为从左至右，Collective的联合创始人：CTO Bugra Akcay、CEO Hooman Radar和CPO Ugur Kaner。
note_en:
In fact, exactly one year ago, the Freelancers Union and Upwork, a digital platform for freelancers, released a report estimating that  35% of the U.S. workforce had begun freelancing. With COVID-19 still making its way around the country and globe, prompting massive and continued job dislocation for many tens of millions  of people, that percentage is likely to rise quickly.
 Unsurprisingly, savvy startups see the economic power of these individuals — many of whom aren’t interested in managing anyone or anything other than the steady growth of their own businesses. A case in point is  Collective, a 2.5-year-old, 20-person San Francisco-based startup that’s been quietly building back office services like tax preparation and bookkeeping for what it dubs “business of one” owners, and which just closed on $8.65 million in seed funding.
  General Catalyst   and QED Investors co-led the round, along with a string or renowned angel investors, including Uber cofounder Garrett Camp, Figma founder Dylan Field, and Doordash executive Gokul Rajaram.
 We talked yesterday with cofounder and CEO  Hooman Radfar   about Collective’s mission to “empower, support and connect the self-employed community” — and what, exactly, it’s proposing.
 TC: You previously founded a company and, even  before it sold to Oracle in 2016, you had jumped over to VC, working with Garrett Camp at his startup studio  Expa. Why shift back into founder mode?
 HR: What I saw throughout across AddThis and Expa and my angel investing is that managing finances is hard. Accounting, taxes, compliance — all that set-up as a small business is annoying.
 Two years ago, [Collective cofounder] Uger [Kaner] came into Expa and he basically pitched me on a startup-in-a-box-type program that we were talking about building from an incubation perspective, but [with more of a pointed focus on back office issues]. He’s an immigrant like me, and because he didn’t quite understand the system, he wound up having tax penalties — penalties that are even worse when you’re a freelancer. Some startups have come up with a  bespoke version of what we offer, but we were like, ‘Why do they have to do it?’ These are commodities, but if you put them together in a platform, they can can be powerful.
 TC: So is what you’ve created proprietary or are you working with third parties?
 HR: Both. We’re an online concierge that’s focused on the back office as the core, meaning accounting and tax services. We also form an S Corp for you because you can save a lot of money [compared with forming a business as an LLC, which features different tax requirements]. So there’s an integration layer plus a dashboard on top of that. If you’re an S Corp, you need to have payroll, so we have partnership with Gusto that comes with your subscription. We have a partnership with Quickbooks. We work with a third party on compliance. Our vision is to make this easy for you and to set this on autopilot because we understand that time is literally money.
  For taxes, accounting, business banking, and payroll, for the core package, it’s $200 a month. We are piloting bookkeeping and a fuller service package that’s probably [representative of] the direction we’ll head over time, and that will be an additional fee.
 TC: How can you persuade these businesses of one that it’s worth that cost?
 HR: There are almost three million people in the U.S. who [employ only themselves and] are making more than $100,000 a year and if you think about how many of these [different products] they are already using, it’s a great deal. Quickbooks and Gusto is cheaper with us. You see savings through expensing. The magic is really running your S Corp the right way. Part of that is normal income tax, but you also have a distribution and it’s taxed differently than an income — it’s taxed less. So we pull in salary data and look at expenses and across states, and say, ‘This is what we’d recommend to you based on how your cash flow is coming in, so you recognize this distribution in a compliant way.’
 TC: Interesting about this useful data that you’ll be amassing from your customers. How might you use it?
 HR: Our first concern is making sure the right people are seeing it [meaning we’re focused on privacy]. But there’s a lot we can do with the aggregation of that data once we’ve earned the right to use it. Among the things we could do, theoretically, including creating a new level of scoring. If you’re a business of one, for example, it’s very difficult to get mortgages and loans, because credit agencies don’t have the tools to assess you. But if we have your financial history for years, can we represent that you’re a great person, you have a great business.
 Another interesting direction as we reach more members — we’ll get to 2,000 soon — would be to use our power as a collective to get our members less expensive insurance, [help facilitate] credit, [help them with a] 401(k).
 TC: There are a lot of other things you can get into presumably, too, from project management to graphic design . . .
 HR: Right now, we’re want to make sure our core service is nailed.
 Think about the transparency and peace of mind that Uber brought to ride-sharing, or that Uber Eats brings to food delivery. You know when something is cooking, when it’s on its way, when it’s arriving. We’ve gotten used to that level of transparency and accountability with so many things, but when it comes to accounting, it’s not there and that’s crazy. We want to change that.
 TC: Going after “businesses of one” means you’re addressing a highly fragmented market. What kinds of partnerships are you striking to reach potential customers?
 HR: We’re having those conversations now, but you can imagine neo banks make sense, along with vertical marketplaces for nurses and doctors and realtors and writers. There are a lot of possibilities.
 Pictured, left to right, Collective’s cofounders: CTO Bugra Akcay, CEO Hooman Radfar, and CPO Ugur Kaner.
title: 专为“一家企业”提供服务的后台办公平台Collect刚刚获得了一轮丰厚的种子融资。
title_en: Collective, a back office platform that caters to ‘businesses of one,’ just landed a hefty seed round

note:
大多数观察家认为巴塞罗那第一，马德里第二，但差距似乎每年都在缩小。巴塞罗那受益于吸引外籍人士寻找阳光、海滩和生活方式，这些人往往会产生更多具有国际意识的初创企业。
马德里的初创企业主要集中在西班牙或拉丁美洲，但本质上已经变得越来越国际化。虽然不是本次调查的一部分，但我们预计瓦伦西亚将于明年加入，因为城市当局一直在全力以赴吸引企业家和投资者。
西班牙的整体生态系统总体上不如英国、法国、瑞典和德国的生态系统成熟，但它一直在快速改善。最近，西班牙的企业家已经不再模仿成功，转而追求创新技术。
金融危机后，西班牙政府支持初创企业的创建，于2017年推出了Fond-ICO global，这是一只15亿欧元的基金，当年向市场投入了8亿欧元。三年后，移动最快的行业是科技行业。2018年，西班牙统计了4115家活跃的初创企业，报告为150秒。巴塞罗那的初创企业和支持系统蓬勃发展，2015年至2019年期间，那里的公司筹集了27亿欧元，几乎是马德里的两倍(根据Dealroom的数据)。
在这项调查的前半部分，我们询问了18家西班牙初创企业投资者他们正在跟踪的趋势，调查分为两部分，我们联系了以下风投公司：
一般来说，你对投资哪些趋势最感兴趣？医疗保健行业的基础设施需求。
有没有一些你希望在这个行业里看到但没有的初创公司？现在有哪些被忽视的机会？我们在数据流动性、硅胶试验、生物技术制造…中看到了机遇。其使能技术可能已经从信息技术和半导体行业中存在。
一般说来，你在下一笔投资中希望得到什么？我们一直做的是：巨大的未得到满足的需求，对医疗保健利益相关者生态系统的深刻理解，正确的技术解决方案，一个我们喜欢合作的团队。
在这一点上，对于一家新的创业公司来说，哪些领域要么是过饱和的，要么是太难竞争的？您还担心或担心哪些其他类型的产品/服务？远程医疗。
与其他创业中心(或其他地方)相比，你在当地生态系统上的投资集中了多少？超过50%？较少?。当地生态系统：占世界其他地区的10%：90%。
你所在的城市和地区的哪些行业似乎处于有利地位，可以长期蓬勃发展，或者不会？你对哪些公司感到兴奋(你的投资组合是否如此)，哪些创始人？我们只投资于HealthTech。因此，答案是：HealthTech：)。
其他城市的投资者应该如何看待你所在城市的整体投资环境和机会？他们都认为我们的气候很好。毕竟，这里是巴塞罗那。特别是在投资环境方面，我相信很少有国际投资者能欣赏到这个城市为创业和发展公司提供的机会的全方位和重要性。
你预计未来几年会有更多的创始人来自大城市以外的地区吗？由于流行病和挥之不去的担忧，加上远程工作的吸引力，初创中心会流失人员吗？不怎么有意思。我认为大多数公司将继续在主要枢纽设立总部，但他们的团队将更加分散。而传统上相对于普通嫌疑人处于劣势的枢纽将发现自己不那么重要。
由于新冠肺炎的原因，您投资的哪些行业领域看起来更弱，或者更容易受到消费者和企业行为潜在变化的影响？在这个史无前例的时代，初创企业可能会利用哪些机会？我们是专业的HealthTech投资者。到目前为止，我们所有的投资都是向医疗机构销售的B2B公司。
新冠肺炎对您的投资策略有何影响？在你的投资组合中，创始人最大的担忧是什么？你现在对你投资组合中的初创公司有什么建议？我们决定增加我们的储备，有更多的资本来支持我们的投资组合公司在接下来的几轮中。更多信息，请看这里。
在过去一个月左右的时间里，是什么时刻给了你希望？这可以是专业的，也可以是个人的，或者两者兼而有之。我的团队很棒。有他们在我身边，我从未失去希望。
您还有其他想法要与TechCrunch读者分享吗？我知道2020年是个悲剧，但是…。看到每个人最终都参与到重要的对话中(医疗保健、科学、公共卫生、政治、平等、多样性)，这难道不是一件事吗？
note_en:
Most observers place Barcelona first and  Madrid   second, but the gap appears to close every year. Barcelona has benefitted from attracting expats in search of sun, beach and lifestyle who tend to produce more internationally minded startups.
 Madrid’s startups have predominantly been Spain or Latin America-focused, but have become increasingly international in nature. Although not part of this survey, we expect Valencia to join next year, as city authorities have been going all-out to attract entrepreneurs and investors.
 The overall Spanish ecosystem is generally less mature than those in the U.K., France, Sweden and Germany, but it has been improving at a fast clip. More recently, entrepreneurs in Spain have moved away from emulating success in pursuit of innovative technologies.
 Following the financial crisis, the Spanish government supported the creation of startups with the launch of FOND-ICO GLOBAL, a €1.5 billion fund-of-funds in 2017, which put €800 million into the market that year. Three years later, the fastest-moving sector is tech. In 2018, Spain counted 4,115 active startups,  reported 150sec. Barcelona has seen a boom in startups and support systems, with companies based there raising €2.7 billion between 2015 and 2019, almost doubling Madrid’s figure (according to Dealroom).
 In the first half of a two-part survey that asks 18 Spain-based startup investors about the trends they’re tracking, we reached out to the following VCs:
   What trends are you most excited about investing in, generally? Infrastructural needs of the healthcare industry.
 Are there startups that you wish you would see in the industry but don’t? What are some overlooked opportunities right now? We see opportunities in data liquidity, in silico trials, biotech manufacturing … for which enabling technologies may already exist from the information technology and semiconductor industry.
 What are you looking for in your next investment, in general? What we always do: Great unmet need, deep understanding of healthcare stakeholder ecosystem, the right technology solution, a team we love to work with.
 Which areas are either oversaturated or would be too hard to compete in at this point for a new startup? What other types of products/services are you wary or concerned about? Telemedicine.
 How much are you focused on investing in your local ecosystem versus other startup hubs (or everywhere) in general? More than 50%? Less? Local ecosystem: 10% Rest of the world: 90%.
 Which industries in your city and region seem well-positioned to thrive, or not, long term? What are companies you are excited about (your portfolio or not), which founders? We only invest in healthtech. So, the answer is: healthtech :)
 How should investors in other cities think about the overall investment climate and opportunities in your city? They all think we have a wonderful climate. After all, it’s Barcelona. Regarding the investment climate in particular, I believe too few international investors appreciate the full spectrum and significance of the opportunities that this city affords for starting and scaling a company.
 Do you expect to see a surge in more founders coming from geographies outside major cities in the years to come, with startup hubs losing people due to the pandemic and lingering concerns, plus the attraction of remote work? Not really. I think most companies will continue to have HQs in the major hubs, but their teams are going to be more distributed. And hubs that were traditionally at disadvantage over the usual suspects will find themselves less so.
 Which industry segments that you invest in look weaker or more exposed to potential shifts in consumer and business behavior because of COVID-19? What are the opportunities startups may be able to tap into during these unprecedented times? We are specialized healthtech investors. All our investments to date are B2B companies selling to healthcare organizations.
 How has COVID-19 impacted your investment strategy? What are the biggest worries of the founders in your portfolio? What is your advice to startups in your portfolio right now? We decided to increase our reserves, to have more capital to support our portfolio companies in follow-on rounds. For more, s ee  here .
 What is a moment that has given you hope in the last month or so? This can be professional, personal or a mix of the two. My team is amazing. With them by my side, I never lost hope.
 Any other thoughts you want to share with TechCrunch readers? I know 2020 is a tragedy but … Isn’t it something to see everyone finally engaged in the conversations that matter (healthcare, science, public health, politics, equality, diversity).

亚马逊目前占美国电子商务销售额的近40%，它通过向外部购物网站出售支付服务和其他技术，从更多的在线购物中提成。现在，这家在线零售巨头也在想方设法从实体购物中分一杯羹-它希望顾客真的能伸出援手。
亚马逊周二发布了一项名为Amazon One的新生物识别技术，允许购物者在商店付款，方法是当他们走进门或结账时，将手掌放在扫描设备上进行支付。当他们第一次注册使用这项技术时，客户将扫描他们的手掌，并在终端插入支付卡；之后，他们就可以简单地用手支付。手扫描技术不仅适用于亚马逊自己的商店，该公司还希望将其出售给其他零售商，包括竞争对手。
亚马逊副总裁迪利普·库马尔周一在接受Recode采访时表示，从周二开始，这项技术将在该公司位于华盛顿州西雅图的两家Amazon Go无收银员便利店的入口处上市，并将在未来推广到该连锁店的20多家门店。Recode在去年12月报道称，亚马逊已经为这种手工支付技术提交了专利申请。
这项技术也可能出现在全食超市(Whole Foods)的门店中，亚马逊在一份新闻稿中暗示，未来几个月将在亚马逊Go门店以外的其他门店引入手掌支付。库马尔不愿对全食超市(Whole Foods)的潜在实施发表评论，尽管“纽约邮报”(New York Post)一年前曾报道过，这样的计划正在酝酿之中。
但这位亚马逊高管确实明确表示，该公司希望将这项技术出售给其他零售商，就像今年早些时候开始的“Just Walk Out”技术一样。“Just Walk Out”技术是相机、传感器和计算机视觉软件的鸡尾酒，为Amazon Go商店提供动力。库马尔说，亚马逊向其他零售商推销One是直截了当的：减少顾客在结账时的摩擦，从而缩短排队时间，增加沿途得到服务的购物者人数。
亚马逊计划将这两项自主研发的技术授权给其他零售商，无论是不是竞争对手，这才是真正的故事：亚马逊并不满足于电子商务的主导地位；它希望在实体零售世界赚取更多交易的一部分，在实体零售世界，80%左右的商业仍发生在美国。因此，它正在打造一套未来主义的服务套件，以吸引其他零售商，同时在自己的门店展示这项技术作为案例研究。
一个明显的问题是，零售商是否愿意与这家科技巨头做生意，其中许多零售商认为亚马逊是这样或那样的竞争对手。库马尔指出，亚马逊网络服务(Amazon Web Services)是亚马逊吸引竞争对手的一个例子。亚马逊网络服务是该公司价值400亿美元的部门，将计算能力、数据存储和无数软件功能出租给大大小小的互联网公司。
亚马逊将收集Amazon One客户使用支付选项时在哪里购物的数据，但它不会知道购物者购买了什么，也不知道他们在第三方零售店内消费了多少。亚马逊的一位发言人表示，公司“没有计划将来自第三方地点的交易信息用于亚马逊广告或其他目的”，如果消费者愿意，他们可以注册这项服务，而不需要将其链接到亚马逊客户账户。
另一个问题是，是否会有足够多的人愿意将自己的手部扫描交给亚马逊，以节省一些结账时间。诚然，在新冠肺炎疫情肆虐的今天，非接触式支付方式可能比一年前更具吸引力。但是，新的支付方式往往面临着严峻的采用挑战，即使在没有生物特征识别的情况下也是如此。生物识别跟踪带来了一系列隐私问题，包括有针对性的黑客攻击或大规模数据泄露的可能性。
亚马逊高管库马尔表示，亚马逊可以引入这项技术的地点越多，就越有价值的客户会找到它，并愿意尝试一下。这就是该公司计划在支付之外推出其他用例的原因。库马尔还表示，亚马逊正在与潜在合作伙伴讨论将手掌扫描与建筑ID联系起来，以取代办公室身份证，或者与体育场或竞技场的活动门票联系起来-这两种设置在全球健康危机期间听起来不是特别有吸引力，但可能是在未来，当聚集在人群中不会构成严重的健康风险时。
这位高管补充说，亚马逊选择手掌扫描而不是其他生物识别选项有几个原因。他说，其中之一是，如果材料曾经泄露，对于一个坏演员来说，仅仅通过查看一个人的手的图像来识别他并不容易。另一个是每个人手的独特性。“即使是同卵双胞胎，他们的手掌结构也有很多不同之处，”他说。一位发言人补充说，图像在扫描时被加密，然后“发送到我们在云中定制的高度安全的区域进行分析和存储。”
对一些人来说，上涨的一面仍然不值得。“人们有多懒，他们会交出手印，这样就不用掏出钱包了？”当我在一次被禁止的餐桌讨论中向我妻子提到这项新技术时，她问道。但苹果的Touch ID指纹扫描技术和面部ID面部扫描技术一开始似乎也有点疯狂--直到事实并非如此。
如果有足够多的顾客信任亚马逊，那么实体零售商将面临一个有趣的两难境地：要么通过与零售业最强大的科技公司结盟来追逐未来，要么坚守现在，希望他们的顾客不会因此而偏离方向。

note_en:
Amazon accounts for nearly 40 percent of e-commerce sales in the US today, and it takes a cut of even more online shopping by selling payments services and other technologies to external shopping sites. Now, the online retail giant is making a play to grab a piece of brick-and-mortar shopping, too — and it wants customers to literally lend a hand to do it.
 Amazon on Tuesday is unveiling a new biometric technology called Amazon One that allows shoppers to pay at stores by placing their palm over a scanning device when they walk in the door or when they check out. The first time they register to use this tech, a customer will scan their palm and insert their payment card at a terminal; after that, they can simply pay with their hand. The hand-scanning tech isn’t just for Amazon’s own stores — the company hopes to sell it to other retailers, including competitors, too.
 The technology will be available at the entrance of two of the company’s  Amazon Go cashierless convenience stores in Seattle, Washington, starting Tuesday, and will roll out to the rest of the chain’s 20-plus stores in the future, Amazon Vice President Dilip Kumar told Recode in an interview Monday. Recode reported in December that  Amazon had filed a patent application for such a hand-payment technology.
 The technology could also show up in Whole Foods stores, with Amazon hinting in a press release that it will introduce palm payments in the coming months at its other stores beyond its Amazon Go locations. Kumar wouldn’t comment on a potential Whole Foods implementation, though the  New York Post reported a year ago that such a plan was in the works.
 But the Amazon executive did make it clear that the company expects to sell the technology to other retailers,  like it began doing earlier this year with its “Just Walk Out” technology — the cocktail of cameras, sensors, and computer vision software that powers Amazon Go stores. Kumar said the Amazon One pitch to other retailers is straightforward: reduce friction for your customers at checkout, thereby shortening lines and increasing how many shoppers are served along the way.
 Amazon’s plan to license these two homegrown technologies to other retailers, whether competitor or not, is the real story: Amazon isn’t satisfied with e-commerce dominance; it wants to earn a cut of more transactions in the physical retail world, where 80-something percent of commerce still takes place in the US. So it’s building out a futuristic suite of services to court other retailers, while showcasing the technology in its own stores as case studies.
 One obvious question is whether retailers, many of which consider Amazon a competitor of one sort or another, will want to do business with the tech giant. Kumar pointed to Amazon Web Services, the company’s $40 billion division that leases computing power, data storage, and myriad software capabilities to internet companies big and small, as an example of an Amazon offering that attracts competitors.
 Amazon will collect data on where Amazon One customers shop when they use the payment option, but it will not know what shoppers purchase or how much they spend inside third-party retail stores. An Amazon spokesperson said the company has “no plans to use transaction information from third-party locations for Amazon advertising or other purposes,” and shoppers can sign up for the service without linking it to an Amazon customer account if they choose.
 Another question is whether enough people will be willing to hand over scans of their hands to Amazon in order to save a bit of time at checkout. It’s true that a no-touch payment method might be more attractive today, during the  Covid-19 pandemic, than even a year ago. But new payment methods often face steep adoption challenges, and that’s even when biometrics aren’t involved. Biometric tracking poses a host of privacy concerns, including the potential of targeted hacking or a mass data breach.
 Kumar, the Amazon executive, said the more locations where Amazon can introduce the technology, the more valuable customers will find it and be willing to give it a try. That’s why the company plans to pitch other use cases beyond payments. Kumar also said Amazon is discussing with potential partners the idea of linking palm scans with building IDs to replace office ID cards, or with event tickets for stadiums or arenas — two settings that don’t sound especially appealing during a global health crisis, but may be in the future when gathering in a crowd won’t pose serious health risks.
 The executive added that Amazon chose palm scans over other biometric options for a few reasons. One, he said, is that it’s not easy for a bad actor to identify a person by simply viewing an image of their hand, if that material ever leaked. Another is the uniqueness of each person’s hand. “Even identical twins have many differences in their palm structure,” he said. A spokesperson added that the images are encrypted when scanned, and then “sent to a highly secure area we custom-built in the cloud for analysis and storage.”
 To some, the upside still won’t be worth it. “How lazy are people that they will hand over their handprints so they don’t have to take out their wallet?” my wife asked when I mentioned the new technology to her in an embargoed dinner-table discussion. But Apple’s Touch ID fingerprint-scanning tech and its Face ID face-scanning tech also seemed a little crazy at first — until they weren’t.
 And if enough customers trust Amazon with the trade-off, physical retailers will face an interesting dilemma: chase the future by aligning with the most powerful tech company in retail, or stick to the present and hope their customers don't stray as a result.

这三款芯片之所以脱颖而出，是因为它们都有内置的安全功能。在这种情况下，“安全”意味着这些芯片具有额外的功能，确保每项计算基本上都经过双重检查。
传统上，ARM为其CPU提供了两种模式。在“拆分模式”下，所有内核都独立工作，只是时不时地离线，以便进行快速的健全性检查。这对于安全性要求较低或没有安全性要求的应用程序非常有效，因为内核可以在接近其最高性能的情况下运行。
在“锁定模式”下，内核成对运行，它们的操作相互交叉检查。这有助于这些芯片满足各种汽车安全要求，但也带来了明显的性能损失，因为您只能使用一半的内核。
今天，该公司为其CPU推出了新的混合模式，在只需要中等故障检测的高性能使用案例中，这种模式结合了两个领域的最佳效果。它允许内核仍然以拆分模式运行，但是集成内核的共享集群逻辑现在以锁定模式运行。这提供了锁定模式的安全机制-只是在不同的层-具有拆分模式的性能。
对于新的AE版本的马里GPU，ARM正在引入它所谓的“灵活分区”，这使得根据需要在不同的工作负载之间拆分各种GPU内核变得更容易。这意味着地图等功能可以在一个分区中运行，独立于司机监控或运行仪表组等安全功能。
传统上，ARM将这些AE品牌的设计瞄准汽车行业。“AE”实际上过去是“自动增强的”的意思。然而，现在它的目标是自动驾驶系统更广阔的市场。
“我们引入了这个AE(知识产权)。ARM汽车业务副总裁Chet Babla告诉我：“它最初指的是‘汽车增强型’，因此它在汽车市场上具有特定的功能、性能和安全性。“但快进到今天，我们意识到，在与工业OEM和计算要求以及他们的安全要求交谈时，他们说，‘实际上，你们在汽车领域所做的事情非常适用于我们面临的计算和安全挑战。’”
虽然ARM对NVIDIA以400亿美元收购的交易相对保持沉默，目前仍在进行监管程序，但值得注意的是，两家公司都将目光投向了这个自主系统市场，NVIDIA为自动机器人提供了自己的平台，例如，它使用了Jetson AGX，除了NVIDIA自己的GPU之外，它还使用了ARM CPU。看起来这一点短期内不会改变。
“需要强大的新处理能力，才能使未来的自动车辆和机器成为可能。作为新型ARM Cortex-A78AE的主要合作伙伴，NVIDIA通过我们的下一代NVIDIA Orin SoC提供这些EDGE人工智能系统所需的高级性能和安全性，“NVIDIA负责硬件开发的高级副总裁Gary Hicok说。
note_en:
What makes all three of these chips stand out is that they have built-in safety features. ‘Safety,’ in this context, means that the chips feature additional capabilities that ensure that every calculation is essentially double-checked.
 Traditionally, Arm has offered two modes for its CPU. In ‘split mode,’ all cores work independently and only go offline every now and then for quick sanity checks. This works well for applications with low or no safety requirements since the cores can run at close to their maximum performance.
   In ‘locked mode,’ cores run in pairs and their operations are cross-checked against each other. This helps these chips satisfy various  automotive safety requirements, but comes with an obvious performance penalty as you can only use half the cores.
 Today, the company introduced its new hybrid mode for its CPUs, which combines the best of both worlds for high-performance use cases where only medium failure detection is needed. It allows the cores to still run in split mode, but the shared cluster logic, which integrates the cores, now runs in lock mode. That provides the safety mechanisms of lock mode — just at a different layer — with the performance of split mode.
 For the new AE-version of the Mali GPU, Arm is introducing what it calls ‘flexible partitioning,’ which makes it easier to split the various GPU cores between workloads as needed. That means features like maps can run in one partition, separate from safety features like driver monitoring or running the instrument cluster.
 Traditionally, Arm targeted these AE-branded designs at the automotive industry. ‘AE’ actually used to stand for ‘automative enhanced.’ Now, however, it is targeting the broader market for autonomous systems.
 “We introduced this AE [intellectual property]. It was referring to ‘automotive enhanced,’ originally, and so it has specific features, performance, safety, for the automotive market,” Arm’s VP of its automotive business, Chet Babla, told me. “But fast forward to today and what we’ve realized that in talking to industrial OEMs and the compute requirements, the safety requirements they have, they’ve said, ‘actually, what you’re doing in the automotive space is very applicable to the compute and safety challenges that we face.'”
 While Arm is remaining relatively quiet about its $40 billion acquisition by Nvidia, which is still going the regulatory process, it’s worth noting that both companies have set their eyes on this market for autonomous systems, with Nvidia offering its own platform for autonomous robots, using its  Jetson AGX, for example, which use ARM CPUs in addition to Nvidia’s own GPUs. It looks like that won’t change anytime soon.
 “Powerful new processing capabilities are needed to enable future autonomous vehicles and machines. As a lead partner for the new Arm Cortex-A78AE, NVIDIA delivers the advanced performance and safety these edge AI systems require with our next-generation NVIDIA Orin SoC,” said Gary Hicok, senior vice president of hardware development at NVIDIA.

note:
然而，该公司并没有自己制作这些内容。它把这留给了伙伴组织，包括全国国务卿协会、BallotReady、SignVote和其他几个组织。
例如，BallotReady将用英语和西班牙语为选举指南提供联邦、州和地方各级候选人的详细信息。关于如何在每个州投票的细节由全国国务卿协会提供。MediaWise将提供有关发现错误信息、选举过程以及如何投票的教育视频。
TikTok说，这项努力还将包括为不同情况下的选民提供资源。这包括作为残疾人投票的信息，来自SignVote的信息；作为海外人士的信息，通过联邦投票援助计划；作为学生在校园投票项目的帮助下的信息；以及作为有过去信念的人在恢复您的选票的帮助下的信息。
TikTok将从今天开始在美国推出这份选举指南，在TikTok发现页面和与选举相关的搜索结果中都可以访问到。它还将在与选举有关的视频底部以及经验证的政治候选人发布的视频中展示该指南。
该公司先发制人地解释说，它只是验证账户，以表明账户所有权的真实性，但经过验证的徽章并不表明TikTok的认可。(关于在社交网络上被验证意味着什么的问题，是Twitter多年来一直面临的问题，因为它向白人民族主义者等有争议的人物发放了令人垂涎的经过验证的徽章。)。
尽管TikTok正在与老牌合作伙伴合作，将其选举指南整合在一起，但对该公司而言，现在仅仅以任何方式参与美国政治都是一个相当大胆的选择。随着用户与新的选举指南互动，他们可能会分享更多关于他们政治倾向的信号。
从理论上讲，这些信号可以用来调整他们的个性化推荐，比如TikTok的For You页面上显示的那些推荐。但TikTok也可以将这些信号数据存储在用户的TikTok账户中，以备将来使用。这与Facebook通过解析你的个人资料信息或你“喜欢”的页面来识别你的偏好没有什么不同。
不过，TikTok表示，在该指南的发布过程中，它不会收集任何用户的个人信息，也不会使用这些信号来定制你的体验。
“…。用户必须访问州或非营利性组织的网站，才能获得任何涉及共享他们信息的内容，包括注册投票，“该公司在一份公告中表示。“在我们的应用程序中，与本指南的互动不会影响未来的TikTok体验，例如推荐或广告。”
尽管围绕TikTok的所有权存在争议，但TikTok正在加入许多社交网络的行列，这些社交网络在其应用程序中提供选民指南或开展“滚出选票”运动。
Facebook今年8月向美国用户推出了投票资源，Twitter本月早些时候推出了其选举中心。Snapchat表示，它帮助40万人登记投票。与此同时，YouTube最近开始添加有关邮寄投票的审查信息，以打击错误信息，以及一些鼓励用户注册投票的功能。
就连Tinder也在美国开展促销活动，通过使用将用户引导到在线资源的应用内卡来推动选民登记。
然而，TikTok的选民登记努力可能会产生影响，因为它的Z世代用户群更年轻。几十年来，年轻选民没有出现在投票站。每次选举年，报道都会怀疑今年是否会有所不同。
但特别是TikTok用户，近几个月来一直出于政治动机。一些人通过注册特朗普塔尔萨集会的门票来帮助恶作剧特朗普竞选，而他们并不打算使用门票。他们还将特朗普竞选官方应用程序拖到了最终不得不重新设置App Store评级的地步。许多人在这款应用上对父母的政治选择进行了猛烈抨击。
TikTok的年轻用户将在多大程度上从纸上谈兵的积极分子转变为现实世界的选民-即使他们已经成年-还有待观察。尽管新闻报道集中在左倾的TikToker，但这款应用可以很容易地将你引导到支持特朗普的泡沫中，在那里你会发现大量戴着Maga帽子的青少年。
与美国其他地区一样，TikTok上的青少年也有自己的政治分歧。这意味着，即使TikTok成功地提高了年轻人的投票率，也没有理由相信它一定会以这样或那样的方式影响投票。
note_en:
The company, however, is not producing this content itself. It’s leaving that up to partner organizations, including the  National Association of Secretaries of State,  BallotReady,  SignVote, and several others.
 BallotReady, for example, will power the elections guide with detailed information about the candidates at the federal, state and local level, in both English and Spanish. Details about how to vote in every state are offered by National Association of Secretaries of State.  MediaWise will provide the educational videos about spotting misinformation and the elections process, as well as how to vote.
  The effort will also include resources for voters in different circumstances, TikTok says. This includes information about voting as a person with disabilities, from  SignVote; as someone overseas, through the  Federal Voting Assistance Program; as a student with help from  Campus Vote Project; and as a person with past convictions, with help from  Restore Your Vote.
 TikTok will feature this elections guide starting today in the U.S., where it will be accessible both on the TikTok Discover page and on election-related search results. It will also feature the guide at the bottom of videos relating the elections and on videos posted by verified political candidates, it says.
 The company preemptively explains it only verifies accounts to indicate the authenticity of the account ownership, but the verified badge does not indicate TikTok’s endorsement. (The issue around what it means to be verified on a social network is a problem Twitter has faced for years, as it doled out its coveted verified badges to controversial figures,  like white nationalists.)
   Though TikTok is working with established partners to put its elections guide together, the mere act of involving itself in any way in American politics right now is a fairly bold choice on the company’s part. As users interact with the new elections guide, they could be sharing additional signals about their political leanings.
 In theory, these signals could be used to tweak their personalized recommendations, like those shown on TikTok’s For You page, for example. But TikTok could also store this signal data with the user’s TikTok account for future use. That’s not all that different from how Facebook is able to identify your leanings by parsing your profile information or the Pages you’ve “liked.”
 TikTok, though, says it won’t be collecting any users’ personal information with the launch of the guide, nor will it use the signals to customize your experience.
 “…A user must visit the website for a state or a non-profit for anything that involves sharing their information, including registering to vote,” the company stated, in an  announcement. “Interactions with this guide in our app have no bearing on future TikTok experiences, such as recommendations or ads.”
  Despite the controversies surrounding TikTok’s ownership, TikTok is joining many social networks that are offering voter guides or  running “get out the vote” campaigns in their apps.
 Facebook rolled out voting resources to U.S. users this August, and  Twitter debuted its Elections hub earlier this month. Snapchat said  it helped 400,000 people get registered to vote. YouTube, meanwhile, recently began  adding vetted information about mail-in voting to counter misinformation along with a few features that encourage users to register to vote.
 Even Tinder is running a promotion in the U.S. to drive voter registration through the use of in-app cards that direct users to online resources.
  However, TikTok’s voter registration efforts could be impactful because of its younger, Gen Z user base. For decades, youth voters fail show up at the polls. And every election year , reports wonder if this year will be any different.
 But TikTok users, in particular, have been politically motivated in recent months. Some  helped to prank the Trump campaign by registering for tickets to Trump’s Tulsa rally, which they didn’t intend to use. They also  trolled the official Trump campaign app to the point that it finally had to reset its App Store ratings. And many went on the app  to troll their parents’ political choices.
 To what extent TikTok’s young users will transition from being armchair activists to real-world voters –if they’re even of age — remains to be seen. And though news coverage has focused on left-leaning TikToker’s, the app can easily direct you to the pro-Trump bubble where you’ll find plenty of MAGA hat-sporting teens.
 Like the rest of the U.S., teens on TikTok have their own political divides. That means even if TikTok is successful in boosting youth voter turnout, there’s no reason to believe it will necessarily help sway the vote one way or the other.
title: TikTok在其应用程序中推出了美国选举指南，与BallotReady、SignVote和其他公司合作，提供有关候选人、如何投票等方面的信息
title_en: TikTok launches a US elections guide in its app, partnering with BallotReady, SignVote, and others to provide info on candidates, how to vote, and more

路透布鲁塞尔8月23日-知情人士称，Alphabet旗下谷歌斥资21亿美元收购健身跟踪器制造商Fitbit，在可穿戴技术市场与苹果和三星展开较量，势将获得欧盟反垄断批准。
周二，全球最受欢迎的互联网搜索引擎百度(Google)向欧盟委员会(European Commission)提出了新的让步，以消除人们对该交易可能巩固谷歌(Google)在线广告实力并提升其数据宝库的担忧。
谷歌表示，已提出限制Fitbit数据用于谷歌广告，并将加强对这一过程的监控，这证实了路透社的一篇报道。这一报价是基于7月份的一份提案。
谷歌在一份声明中表示：“我们还在正式确认我们长期以来的承诺，即支持其他可穿戴设备制造商使用Android系统，并继续允许Fitbit用户通过API(应用程序编程接口)连接到第三方服务，如果他们愿意的话。”
在用户同意的情况下，第三方还可以继续访问Fitbit用户的数据。
知情人士说，路透社(Reuters)早些时候报道的这些让步将为交易获得批准扫清道路。
欧盟委员会拒绝置评，计划在12月23日之前就这笔交易做出决定。它的决定可能会早于最后期限。
欧盟竞争执法者现在将征求竞争对手和客户的反馈，然后再决定是接受谷歌的让步，要求更多，还是明确或阻止细节。
上个月，谷歌拒绝了谷歌的承诺，即不会将健身跟踪器的数据用于广告目的，以解决竞争担忧，称这是不够的。
市场研究公司国际数据公司(International Data Corp)的数据显示，截至2020年第一季度，Fitbit曾是可穿戴设备市场的领头羊，在全球可穿戴设备市场占有3%的份额，落后于苹果29.3%的份额，以及小米、三星和华为。
据一位知情人士透露，欧盟委员会最近询问了谷歌的竞争对手和客户有关互操作性问题，谷歌可以采取哪些技术措施来阻止与Fitbit的竞争，以增加其销售额，以及什么可能促使其这样做。
这位知情人士说，该公司还询问了与数字医疗相关的问题，以及谷歌需要的数据类型，以及这家美国公司可以在哪里获得这些数据，这表明可能需要在这两个领域做出让步。
note_en:
BRUSSELS (Reuters) - Alphabet’s Google is set to win EU antitrust approval for its $2.1 billion purchase of fitness tracker maker Fitbit to take on Apple and Samsung in the wearable technology market, people familiar with the matter said.
  The world’s most popular internet search engine on Tuesday offered fresh concessions to the European Commission in a bid to address concerns the deal could entrench Google’s power in online advertising and boost its trove of data.
 Google said it had offered to restrict the use of Fitbit data for Google ads and would also tighten the monitoring of that process, confirming a Reuters report. The offer is based on a July proposal.
 “We’re also formalizing our longstanding commitment to supporting other wearable manufacturers on Android and to continue to allow Fitbit users to connect to third party services via APIs (application programming interfaces) if they want to,” Google said in a statement.
  Third parties will also continue to have access to Fitbit users’ data, with users’ consent.
 The concessions, reported earlier by Reuters, are set to clear the way for the deal to be approved, the people said.
 The Commission, which is scheduled to decide on the deal by Dec. 23, declined to comment. Its decision could come earlier than the deadline.
  The EU competition enforcer will now seek feedback from rivals and customers before deciding whether to accept Google’s concessions, demand more, or either clear or block the detail.
 Last month, it rejected Google’s pledge not to use the fitness tracker’s data for advertising purposes in a bid to address competition concerns, saying that it was insufficient.
   Fitbit, once the leader in the wearable devices market, had a 3% share of the global wearables market as of the first quarter of 2020, lagging behind Apple’s 29.3% share, as well as Xiaomi, Samsung and Huawei, data from market research firm International Data Corp showed.
 The Commission recently asked Google’s rivals and customers about interoperability issues, what technical steps Google could take to foreclose competition to Fitbit to increase its sales, and what could prompt it to do so, according to a person familiar with the matter.
 It also asked about issues related to digital healthcare and the kind of data Google needs, and where the U.S. company could acquire it, the person said, suggesting concessions may be needed in these two areas.
title: 消息人士：欧盟将批准谷歌对Fitbit的收购，此前该公司提出了新的让步，比如不使用Fitbit数据投放广告
title_en: Sources: EU is set to approve Google's Fitbit acquisition after the company offered new concessions, like not using Fitbit data for ads

由于8chan的失踪，支持特朗普的阴谋论变得无家可归。8chan是留言板上的化名中心人物“Q”，他在那里发布了关于吃儿童的撒旦恋童癖者阴谋的神秘线索。埃尔帕索大规模枪击案发生后，留言板被其安全提供商切断了，当8chan的所有者吉姆·沃特金斯(Jim Watkins)正在努力让一个替代网站上线时，一些QAnon的信徒似乎正在失去兴趣。
然后，流行病来袭-随之而来的是新一波错误信息，QAnon可能会将这些错误信息纳入其总体叙述，从关于戴口罩的虚假说法，到关于比尔·盖茨和新冠肺炎疫苗的阴谋论。5月份乔治·弗洛伊德(George Floyd)被杀后爆发的“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)抗议活动，也为QAnon的“面包师”提供了新的素材。这些业余侦探聚集在Facebook的私人群组和聊天室里，解读Q的最新帖子，讨论他们关于全球阴谋的理论。
但新的研究表明，QAnon受到的最大冲击来自所谓的“拯救儿童”运动。它最初是一家合法的反人口贩运慈善机构的筹款活动，但后来被QAnon的信徒劫持，他们利用这场运动传播关于民主党高层和好莱坞精英领导的全球贩卖儿童阴谋的虚假和夸大的说法。这起劫持事件始于7月份，大约在Twitter和Facebook开始打击QAnon账户的同一时间。
康科迪亚大学(Concordia University)研究QAnon的博士生马克-安德烈·阿根廷诺(Marc-André阿根廷)一直在跟踪“拯救儿童”Facebook小组的发展，其中许多小组都是这场运动的软前线。
阿根廷确定了114个自称关注打击人口贩运，但实际上主要是QAnon内容的团体。他发现，自7月份以来，这些团体的成员增加了超过3000%-是的，3000%-这些团体内部的活动也相应地激增。
“在Twitter和Facebook对Qanon采取行动后，拯救儿童会真正重振了社区，”阿根廷先生说。“它给Qanon带来了一个全新的群体。”
阿根廷还发现，QAnon的几个核心内容--比如YouTube上的一段视频“阴谋集团的陨落”(Fall Of Cabal)--在经历了几个月的下滑后，最近几周出现了激增。许多Qanon的信徒认为，这段视频激发了他们对该组织的兴趣。
Facebook、Twitter和其他社交网络试图限制QAnon的传播，关闭了一些与这场运动相关的账户和页面。但是，对于平台执法者来说，“拯救儿童”是一个更加模糊的领域，因为很难分辨出谁真正关心儿童剥削，谁在利用这些担忧散布错误信息。这种含糊不清帮助QAnon的信徒避免了全面镇压，并为他们提供了讨论他们的理论的场所，这些理论不太容易被拿走。
采用“拯救儿童”作为口头禅，在其他几个方面帮助拯救了Qanon。它创造了一种“QAnon Lite”的入口--QAnon的信徒可以公开谈论这个问题，而不会因为希拉里·克林顿(Hillary Clinton)吃婴儿的离奇言论而吓跑潜在的新兵，而且这个问题可能会在专注于育儿、自然健康和其他非政治话题的小组中通过，几乎不会引起人们的注意。
新的、低调的QAnon风格的典型代表是Facebook视频，在视频中，父母对恋童癖者洗脑和掠夺儿童发出警告。研究人员安妮·凯利(Annie Kelly)本月就QAnon对女性的吸引力写了一篇时报专栏文章，她写道，这些视频构成了“引人注目和戏剧性的内容”，“很容易在其他育儿团体中分享，几乎看不到他们的极右翼出身”。
由于停止剥削儿童是一个得到两党广泛同情的问题，QAnon的反人口贩运品牌重塑也允许政客们在没有明确提到这一理论的情况下向QAnon的支持者发出呼吁。此外，在Instagram和TikTok等平台上散布有关儿童性交易的错误信息，让QAnon得以进入更年轻、不那么明确支持特朗普的人群。
“这降低了QAnon追随者的平均年龄，”阿根廷先生说。“在2019年，这主要是一场婴儿潮时期的运动。现在我们看到千禧一代和Z世代加入了我们的行列。“。
阿根廷先生的研究表明，QAnon的“拯救儿童”战略是多么有效。除了在世界各地刺激面对面的集会外，这场运动已经成为Facebook上最强大的力量之一。关于剥削儿童和贩卖人口的报道通常会成为该网站上分享最多的新闻文章之一，一些与Qanon相邻的丑闻--比如Netflix的“Cuties”电影引发的轩然大波--已经在Qanon Facebook的群组中讨论了数周，后来被共和党议员谴责为促进儿童性别化--已经成为主流政治话语。
表达对剥削儿童的担忧没有错，这是真实和有害的。但是QAnon对“拯救儿童”运动的拥护也造成了它自己的伤害。合法的反人口贩运组织报告说，QAnon信徒的电话蜂拥而至，传递虚假和揭穿的提示，迫使这些组织将资源从他们的实际工作中分流出来。QAnon的信徒组织了针对他们指控为恋童癖者的恶毒骚扰运动，包括克里西·泰根(Chrissy Teigen)和艾伦·德杰尼勒斯(Ellen DeGeneres)等名人。
一些QAnon的追随者已经对他们认为正在运行一个地下儿童性贩卖团伙的想象中的“阴谋集团”进行了义务警员的正义行动。去年，一名科罗拉多州的妇女因涉嫌与其他QAnon支持者密谋将她的儿子从寄养中心绑架而被捕。(这名女子辛西娅·阿布库(Cynthia Abcug)已提出无罪抗辩。)。
正如阿根廷先生最近在推特上关于他的发现所指出的那样，有一些证据表明“拯救儿童”的增长可能正在放缓。他追踪的114个群组内的帖子分享在最近几周有所下降，尽管这些群组的成员人数继续上升。Facebook最近的打击行动可能解释了分享减少的部分原因。但这可能是QAnon准备继续前进的证据-该公司需要不断提供新的错误信息和新的叙事来保持其社区的吸引力。
“人们开始厌倦了，”阿根廷先生说。“关于儿童性交易的内容你能分享的就这么多了。”
note_en:
The pro-Trump conspiracy theory had been left homeless by the disappearance of 8chan, the message board where “Q,” its pseudonymous central figure, posted cryptic clues about a cabal of child-eating Satanic pedophiles. The message board had been  cut off by its security provider after the El Paso mass shooting, and while 8chan’s owner, Jim Watkins, was struggling to bring a replacement site online, some QAnon believers appeared to be losing interest.
 Then, the pandemic hit — and with it, a new wave of misinformation that QAnon could incorporate into its overarching narrative, from  false claims about mask-wearing to  conspiracy theories about Bill Gates and a Covid-19 vaccine. The Black Lives Matter protests that erupted after the killing of George Floyd in May also provided new fodder for QAnon’s “bakers” — the amateur sleuths who gather in private Facebook groups and chat rooms to decode Q’s latest posts and discuss their theories about the global cabal.
 But new research suggests that the biggest jolt to QAnon came from the so-called “Save the Children” movement. It started out as a fund-raising campaign for a legitimate anti-trafficking charity, but was then  hijacked by QAnon believers, who used the movement to spread false and exaggerated claims about a global child-trafficking conspiracy led by top Democrats and Hollywood elites. This hijacking began in July, around the same time that Twitter and Facebook began cracking down on QAnon accounts.
 Marc-André Argentino, a doctoral student at Concordia University who studies QAnon, has been tracking the growth of “Save the Children” Facebook groups, many of which operate as soft fronts for the movement.
 Mr. Argentino identified 114 groups that bill themselves as anti-trafficking concerns, but are actually dominated by QAnon content. Since July, he found, these groups have increased their membership by more than 3,000 percent — yes,  3,000 percent — with a corresponding surge in activity within these groups.
 “Save the Children really revitalized the community after Twitter and Facebook took action against QAnon,” Mr. Argentino said. “It’s introduced an entire new population to QAnon.”
  Mr. Argentino also found that traffic to several pieces of core QAnon content — such as “Fall of Cabal,” a YouTube video that many QAnon believers have credited with spurring their interest in the group — has surged in recent weeks, after months of decline.
 Facebook, Twitter and other social networks have tried to limit the spread of QAnon, shutting down some accounts and pages associated with the movement. But “Save the Children” is a fuzzier area for platform enforcers, because it can be difficult to tell who is genuinely concerned about child exploitation and who is taking advantage of those concerns to sow misinformation. That vagueness has helped QAnon believers avoid a total crackdown, and has given them venues to discuss their theories that aren’t as vulnerable to being taken away.
 Adopting “Save the Children” as a mantra helped save QAnon in several other ways. It created a kind of “QAnon Lite” on-ramp — an issue QAnon believers could talk about openly without scaring off potential recruits with bizarre claims about Hillary Clinton eating babies, and one that could pass nearly unnoticed in groups devoted to parenting, natural health and other nonpolitical topics.
 Typical of the new, understated QAnon style are Facebook videos in which parents sound the alarm about pedophiles brainwashing and preying on children. These videos, wrote Annie Kelly, a researcher who wrote a Times op-ed about  QAnon’s appeal to women this month, make for “compelling and dramatic content” that is “easily shared in other parenting groups with little indication of their far-right origins.”
 Since stopping child exploitation is an issue that has broad and bipartisan sympathy, QAnon’s anti-trafficking rebranding has also allowed politicians to appeal to QAnon supporters without explicitly mentioning the theory. And seeding misinformation about child sex trafficking on platforms like Instagram and TikTok has allowed QAnon to tap into a younger and less explicitly pro-Trump demographic.
 “It’s bringing down the average age of a QAnon follower,” Mr. Argentino said. “In 2019, this was mainly a boomer movement. Now we’re seeing millennials and Gen Z getting on board.”
 Mr. Argentino’s research shows just how effective QAnon’s “Save the Children” pivot has been. In addition to spurring in-person rallies all over the world, the movement has become one of the most potent forces on Facebook. Stories about child exploitation and human trafficking routinely end up being among the most-shared news articles on the site, and some QAnon-adjacent scandals — such as  the uproar over Netflix’s “Cuties” film, which was discussed for weeks inside QAnon Facebook groups before it was condemned by Republican lawmakers as promoting child sexualization — have crossed over into mainstream political discourse.
 There is nothing wrong with expressing concerns about child exploitation, which is real and harmful. But QAnon’s embrace of the “Save the Children” movement has created its own harms. Legitimate anti-trafficking groups have reported being flooded with calls from QAnon believers passing on false and debunked tips, forcing the groups to divert resources away from their actual work. QAnon believers have organized virulent harassment campaigns against people they accuse of being pedophiles, including celebrities like Chrissy Teigen and Ellen DeGeneres.
 And some QAnon followers have pursued acts of vigilante justice against the imagined “cabal” they believe is running an underground child sex-trafficking ring. Last year, a Colorado woman was arrested on suspicion of plotting with other QAnon supporters to have her son kidnapped from foster care. (The woman, Cynthia Abcug, has  pleaded not guilty.)
 As Mr. Argentino points out in a  recent Twitter thread about his findings, there is some evidence that the growth of “Save the Children” may be slowing down. Sharing of posts inside the 114 groups he tracked has declined in recent weeks, even as membership in the groups has continued to rise. Facebook’s recent crackdowns may explain part of the falloff in sharing. But it could be evidence that QAnon — which needs a constant supply of fresh misinformation and new narratives to keep its community hooked — is preparing to move on.
 “People are getting bored,” Mr. Argentino said. “There’s only so much content about child sex trafficking that you can share.”
title: 分析显示，在8chan失踪后，QAnon的支持者在7月份劫持了#SavetheChildren，这一举动大幅增加了追随者和覆盖面
title_en: Analysis shows that QAnon supporters' move to hijack #SaveTheChildren in July, following 8chan's disappearance, has substantially increased followers and reach

note:
在第一场总统辩论前夕，拜登竞选团队向Facebook施压，要求其删除特朗普总统的帖子，并抨击这家社交媒体公司是美国最大的关于投票过程的虚假信息的宣传者。
重要原因：通过公开升级冲突，这场运动正在向Facebook施压，要求其更积极地执行针对错误信息的政策。
细节：我们非但没有看到进步，反而看到了倒退，在Axios获得的一封三页长的信中，竞选经理珍妮·狄龙(Jen O&39；Malley Dillon)给Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)写了一封信。
信中说，Facebook对未来行动的持续承诺只不过是不采取行动的借口。
在接下来的36天里，当这些故障发生时，我们会把它们叫出来。
另一方面：Facebook公司发言人安迪·斯通(Andy Stone)在一份声明中告诉Axios：虽然许多共和党人认为我们应该采取一种做法，但许多民主党人认为我们应该采取相反的做法。
他说：我们一直面临共和党人的批评，批评他们对保守派和民主党人有偏见，因为他们没有采取更多措施限制完全相同的内容。
我们已经制定了规则来保护选举的完整性和言论自由，我们将继续公正地执行这些规则。&#34；-。
大局：特朗普长期以来一直使用Facebook和Twitter直接与支持者交谈，鼓励他们投票。
特朗普已经发布信息，告诉支持者去你的投票站看看你的邮寄选票是否已经记录在案，如果他们的选票还没有清点，就要求亲自投票。
最近几周，总统还转向Facebook，放大了人们对邮寄选票完整性的怀疑。
拜登竞选团队表示，这些帖子鼓励非法行为，应该删除-并质疑为什么Facebook会有所保留。
信中写道，到目前为止，特朗普清楚地明白，Facebook不会强迫他遵守他们明确声明的政策。
倒叙：6月份，拜登的竞选团队发表了一份请愿书和一封致扎克伯格的信，要求这家社交媒体巨头实施更严厉的虚假信息规则，并追究包括特朗普总统在内的政客散布谎言的责任。
note_en:
On the eve of the first presidential debate, the Biden campaign is pressing Facebook to remove posts by President Trump — and slamming the social media company as "the nation’s foremost propagator of disinformation about the voting process."
 Why it matters: By publicly escalating the conflict, the campaign is pressing Facebook to enforce its policies against misinformation more aggressively.
 Details: "Rather than seeing progress, we have seen regression," campaign manager Jen O'Malley Dillon wrote to Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg in a three-page letter obtained by Axios.
 "Facebook’s continued promise of future action is serving as nothing more than an excuse for inaction," the letter says.
 "We will be calling out those failures as they occur over the coming 36 days."
 The other side: Andy Stone, a Facebook company spokesperson, tells Axios in a statement: "While many Republicans think we should take one course, many Democrats think we should do the exact opposite.
 "We’ve faced criticism from Republicans for being biased against conservatives and Democrats for not taking more steps to restrict the exact same content," he said.
 "We have rules in place to protect the integrity of the election and free expression, and we will continue to apply them impartially." -
 The big picture: Trump has long used Facebook as well as Twitter to talk directly to supporters and encourage them to vote.
 Trump has posted messages telling supporters to "go to your Polling Place to see whether or not your Mail In Vote has been Tabulated" and to demand to vote in person if their ballots haven't been counted.
 In recent weeks, the president has also turned to Facebook to amplify doubts about the integrity of mail-in ballots.
 The Biden campaign says that these posts encourage illegal behavior and should be taken down — and questions why Facebook is holding back.
 "By now Mr. Trump clearly understands that Facebook will not hold him to their clearly stated policies," the letter states.
 Flashback:  In June, Biden's campaign  published a petition and a letter to Zuckerberg demanding that the social media giant implement stronger misinformation rules and hold politicians, including President Trump, accountable for spreading lies.
title: 拜登竞选团队称Facebook是“全国最主要的关于投票过程的虚假信息的宣传者”，因为特朗普一直在攻击选举的完整性
title_en: Biden campaign calls Facebook the “nation's foremost propagator of disinformation about the voting process” as Trump keeps attacking the election's integrity

D-Wave今天发布了其下一代量子计算平台，可通过其Leap量子云服务获得。该公司称Advantage是“第一台为商业目的制造的量子计算机”。本着这一思路，D-Wave今天还推出了Launch，这是一个为想要开始构建混合量子应用的企业提供的快速启动计划。
D-Wave首席执行官艾伦·巴拉茨告诉VentureBeat：“Advantage量子计算机是第一台从头开始设计和开发的支持商业应用的量子计算机。”我们将其设计为能够处理大型、复杂的商业应用程序，并能够支持这些应用程序在生产环境中运行。世界上没有其他任何地方的量子计算机可以像这台量子计算机那样解决规模和复杂性的问题。它确实是您唯一可以在其上运行真正的业务应用程序的平台。其他量子计算机主要是原型。你可以做实验，进行小的概念验证，但它们都不能支持我们所能支持的规模的应用程序。“。
量子计算利用量子比特(与只能处于0或1状态的比特不同，量子比特也可以处于这两种状态的叠加)来执行对经典计算机来说困难得多或根本不可行的计算。总部设在加拿大伯纳比的D-Wave是第一家销售商用量子计算机的公司，这种计算机是为使用量子退火而建造的。但是D-Wave已经不卖量子计算机了。Advantage及其5000多个量子比特(该公司2000Q系统中的2000个量子比特)只能通过云获得。(这意味着通过Leap或亚马逊Braket这样的合作伙伴。)。
如果你被“超过5,000个量子比特”这一部分搞糊涂了，你并不孤单。更多的量子比特通常意味着构建商业量子应用的更大潜力。但是D-Wave并没有给出一个具体的量子比特数作为优势，因为确切的数量在不同的系统中是不同的。
D-Wave的一位发言人告诉VentureBeat：“本质上，D-Wave是在利用Advantage向Leap用户保证5000个量子比特的可用性。”“在每个优势系统中，量子位的实际具体数量因芯片而异。其中一些芯片的量子比特明显超过5000个，而另一些芯片则略接近5000个。但底线是，任何使用Leap的人都将有权完全访问至少5000个量子比特。“。
Advantage还承诺15路量子比特连接，这要归功于一种新的芯片拓扑Pegasus，D-Wave早在2019年2月就详细介绍了这一拓扑结构。(Pegasus的前身Chimera提供了六个相互连接的量子比特。)。将每个量子比特连接到15个其他量子比特，而不是6个，这意味着连接能力增加了2.5倍，这反过来又使得用更少的物理量子比特嵌入更大、更复杂的问题成为可能。
巴拉茨说：“量子比特的数量和这些量子比特之间的连通性决定了你能在量子计算机上自然解决多大的问题。”有了2000量子比特处理器，我们就可以自然地解决100-200个变量范围内的问题。有了优势量子计算机，拥有两倍的量子比特和两倍的连接性，我们可以解决600到800个变量范围内的更多问题。当我们研究了不同类型的问题，并做了一些粗略的计算，结果表明，一般来说，我们在Advantage系统上可以解决的问题大约是我们在2000量子比特处理器上解决的问题的2.6倍。但这不应与使用Advantage量子计算机支持的混合解算器可以解决的大小问题混淆。
D-Wave今天还宣布，其扩展的混合求解器服务将能够处理多达100万个变量的问题(从10000个变量增加)。它将于10月8日在Leap正式上市。离散二次模型(DQM)解算器应该允许企业和开发人员将混合量子计算应用于新的问题类别。DQM解算器不再只接受二进制变量(0或1)的问题，而是使用其他变量集(从1到500的整数、颜色等)，从而扩展了可以运行Advantage的问题类型。D-Wave断言，Advantage和DQM的结合将让企业“第一次运行高性能的、实时的、混合的量子应用程序”。
换句话说，100万个变量意味着要处理大规模的关键业务问题。“现在，有了Advantage系统和混合求解器服务的增强，我们将能够解决多达100万个变量的问题，”巴拉茨说。“这意味着真正能够解决生产规模的商业应用。”
不同的量子计算机往往更善于解决不同的问题，这取决于它们所基于的技术。D-Wave长期以来一直表示，其量子计算机擅长解决优化问题，“而大多数商业问题都是优化问题，”巴拉茨认为。
与2000Q的前身相比，Advantage不能解决不同类型的问题。但再加上DQM和绝对数量的变量，它对企业的用处可能仍然要大得多。
“架构是一样的，”巴拉茨证实。“这两台量子计算机都是退火量子计算机。所以问题的类别，他们能解决的问题的类型都是一样的。只是规模和复杂性不同而已。2000量子位处理器无法解决这些问题，我们的客户需要解决这些问题才能影响他们的业务运营。“。
今年3月，D-Wave将其量子计算机免费提供给冠状病毒研究人员和开发人员。巴拉茨告诉VentureBeat：“通过这个过程，我们了解到，虽然我们有非常好的软件、非常好的工具、非常好的培训，但开发人员和企业仍然需要帮助。”“帮助了解他们可以从量子计算机中受益的最好问题是什么，以及如何最好地制定这些问题，以最大限度地利用量子计算机。”
因此，D-Wave的推出将使该公司的应用专家和一系列精心挑选的合作伙伴公司可供其客户使用。推出的目的是帮助任何人了解如何最好地利用D-Wave的量子系统来支持他们的业务。在D-Wave的网站上填写一张表格，你将被分类，以确定谁可能最有能力提供指导。
巴拉茨说：“要想真正使用量子处理器，你确实需要成为Leap的客户。”“但你不必先成为Leap的客户。我们非常高兴与您合作，帮助您了解量子计算机的好处以及如何使用它。“。
作为发布的一部分，D-Wave将提供“大约10名”自己的员工，外加合作伙伴。
note_en:
D-Wave today launched its next-generation quantum computing platform available via  its Leap quantum cloud service. The company calls Advantage “the first quantum computer built for business.” In that vein, D-Wave today also debuted Launch, a jump-start program for businesses that want to begin building hybrid quantum applications.
 “The Advantage quantum computer is the first quantum computer designed and developed from the ground up to support business applications,” D-Wave CEO Alan Baratz told VentureBeat. “We engineered it to be able to deal with large, complex commercial applications and to be able to support the running of those applications in production environments. There is no other quantum computer anywhere in the world that can solve problems at the scale and complexity that this quantum computer can solve problems. It really is the only one that you can run real business applications on. The other quantum computers are primarily prototypes. You can do experimentation, run small proofs of concept, but none of them can support applications at the scale that we can.”
  Quantum computing leverages qubits (unlike bits that can only be in a state of 0 or 1, qubits can also be in a superposition of the two) to perform computations that would be much more difficult, or simply not feasible, for a classical computer. Based in Burnaby, Canada, D-Wave was the first company to sell commercial quantum computers, which are built to use  quantum annealing. But D-Wave doesn’t sell quantum computers anymore. Advantage and its over 5,000 qubits (up from 2,000 in the company’s 2000Q system) are only available via the cloud. (That means through Leap or a partner like  Amazon Braket.)
  If you’re confused by the “over 5,000 qubits” part, you’re not alone. More qubits typically means more potential for building commercial quantum applications. But D-Wave isn’t giving a specific qubit count for Advantage because the exact number varies between systems.
 “Essentially, D-Wave is guaranteeing the availability of 5,000 qubits to Leap users using Advantage,” a D-Wave spokesperson told VentureBeat. “The actual specific number of qubits varies from chip to chip in each Advantage system. Some of the chips have significantly more than 5,000 qubits, and others are a bit closer to 5,000. But bottom line — anyone using Leap will have full access to at least 5,000 qubits.”
  Advantage also promises 15-way qubit connectivity, thanks to a new chip topology,  Pegasus, which  D-Wave detailed back in February 2019. (Pegasus’ predecessor,  Chimera, offered six connected qubits.) Having each qubit connected to 15 other qubits instead of six translates to 2.5 times more connectivity, which in turn enables the embedding of larger and more complex problems with fewer physical qubits.
 “The combination of the number of qubits and the connectivity between those qubits determines how large a problem you can solve natively on the quantum computer,” Baratz said. “With the 2,000-qubit processor, we could natively solve problems within 100- to 200-variable range. With the Advantage quantum computer, having twice as many qubits and twice as much connectivity, we can solve problems more in the 600- to 800-variable range. As we’ve looked at different types of problems, and done some rough calculations, it comes out to generally we can solve problems about 2.6 times as large on the Advantage system as what we could have solved on the 2000-qubit processor. But that should not be mistaken with the size problem you can solve using the hybrid solver backed up by the Advantage quantum computer.”
  D-Wave today also announced its expanded hybrid solver service will be able to handle problems with up to 1 million variables (up from 10,000 variables). It will be generally available in Leap on October 8. The discrete quadratic model (DQM) solver is supposed to let businesses and developers apply hybrid quantum computing to new problem classes. Instead of accepting problems with only binary variables (0 or 1), the DQM solver uses other variable sets (integers from 1 to 500, colors, etc.), expanding the types of problems that can run on Advantage. D-Wave asserts that Advantage and DQM together will let businesses “run performant, real-time, hybrid quantum applications for the first time.”
 Put another way, 1 million variables means tackling large-scale, business-critical problems. “Now, with the Advantage system and the enhancements to the hybrid solver service, we’ll be able to solve problems with up to 1 million variables,” Baratz said. “That means truly able to solve production-scale commercial applications.”
  Depending on the technology they are built on, different quantum computers tend to be better at solving different problems. D-Wave has long said its quantum computers are good at solving optimization problems, “and most business problems are optimization problems,” Baratz argues.
 Advantage isn’t going to be able to solve different  types of problems, compared to its 2000Q predecessor. But coupled with DQM and the sheer number of variables, it may still be significantly more useful to businesses.
 “The architecture is the same,” Baratz confirmed. “Both of these quantum computers are annealing quantum computers. And so the class of problems, the types of problems they can solve, are the same. It’s just at a different scale and complexity. The 2000-qubit processor just couldn’t solve these problems at the scale that our customers need to solve them in order for them to impact their business operations.”
  In March, D-Wave made its  quantum computers available for free to coronavirus researchers and developers. “Through that process what we learned was that while we have really good software, really good tools, really good training, developers and businesses still need help,” Baratz told VentureBeat. “Help understanding what are the best problems that they can benefit from the quantum computer and how to best formulate those problems to get the most out of the quantum computer.”
 D-Wave Launch will thus make the company’s application experts and a set of handpicked partner companies available to its customers. Launch aims to help anyone understand how to best leverage D-Wave’s quantum systems to support their business. Fill out a form on D-Wave’s website and you will be triaged to determine who might be best able to offer guidance.
 “In order to actually do anything with the quantum processor, you do need to become a Leap customer,” Baratz said. “But you don’t have to first become a Leap customer. We’re perfectly happy to engage with you to help you understand the benefits of the quantum computer and how to use it.”
 D-Wave will make available “about 10” of its own employees as part of Launch, plus partners.
title: D-Wave推出Advantage，这是一项提供5000多量子位量子计算的云服务，以及一项可以处理1M变量的混合解算器服务
title_en: D-Wave launches Advantage, a cloud service that offers access to 5,000+ qubit quantum computing and a hybrid solver service that can handle 1M variables

note:
在Accounts Center中，用户将能够选择打开或关闭单点登录，这是一个身份验证选项，允许您使用Facebook帐户信息登录Instagram或恢复帐户等操作。
在新的设置区，你还可以调整你的故事发布的方式，例如，你是否希望你的故事同时发布到Facebook和Instagram上。
虽然Facebook在发布时尚未推出，但它表示将在今年晚些时候将Facebook Pay添加到账户中心。在美国，你可以在一个地方输入支付信息，然后在Facebook和Instagram上购物时，比如在新的Facebook和Instagram商店里，或者当你捐款时，就可以在Facebook和Instagram上使用它。
Facebook表示，选择使用账户中心的用户不必在整个Facebook上公开使用相同的身份。例如，你可以继续使用Facebook上的个人资料，同时使用Instagram来宣传你的业务或爱好。但对于那些在不同平台上保持相同身份的人来说，这项功能可能更有用，因为你可以做一些事情，比如在不同的应用程序之间同步你的个人资料照片。
然而，这一新功能暴露了Facebook通过其各种应用程序建立的广泛的数据收集操作。在一篇博客文章中，Facebook明确表示，它使用来自整个应用套件的信息来个性化您的体验，包括显示哪些广告。换句话说，即使你在公开场合保持不同的身份，Facebook也在幕后聚合你的数据。这使得它能够保持其在社交领域的市场主导地位，并有可能扼杀新的竞争。这件事一直处于美国政府和其他地方反垄断调查的前沿，这些调查仍在进行中。
在没有监管机构干预的情况下，Facebook并没有放慢让其应用套件更具互操作性的计划。例如，今年夏天，它开始测试Instagram和Messenger聊天的合并。这些努力今天仍在继续。
Facebook表示，新账户中心将于本周开始在Facebook、Instagram和Messenger上进行测试。
note_en:
In Accounts Center, users will be able to optionally turn on or off Single Sign On, an authentication option that  allows you to do things like use your Facebook account information to log into Instagram or to recover your accounts.
   In the new settings area, you’ll also be able to make adjustments to how your Stories post — for instance, whether you want your Stories to  publish to both Facebook and Instagram at the same time.
 Though not available at launch, Facebook says it will add Facebook Pay to the Accounts Center later this year. In the U.S., you’ll then be able to enter your payment information in one place then use it across both Facebook and Instagram when you make purchases, like in the  new Facebook and Instagram Shops, or when you make donations.
 Facebook says users who choose to use Accounts Center won’t have to publicly use the same identity across all of Facebook. You could, for instance, continue to use a personal profile on Facebook while using Instagram to promote your business or hobbies. But the feature will likely be more useful for those who do maintain the same identify across platforms, as you can do things like sync your profile photo across apps.
 The new feature, however, brings to light the extensive data collection operation Facebook has built by way of its various apps. In  a blog post, Facebook clearly states that it uses information from across its suite of apps to personalize your experience, including which ads are shown. In other words, even if you maintain different identities publicly, Facebook is aggregating your data behind the scenes. This allows it to maintain its market dominance in social and potentially stifle new competition. This matter has been at the  forefront of the U.S. government’s antitrust investigations, and elsewhere, which are still ongoing.
 Without intervention from regulators, Facebook isn’t slowing on plans to make its suite of apps ever more interoperable. This summer, for example,  it began testing the merger of Instagram and Messenger chats. Those efforts continue today.
 Facebook says the test of the new Accounts Center will begin this week across Facebook, Instagram and Messenger.
title: Facebook推出了Accounts Center，这是一款用于管理Facebook、Instagram和Messenger中不断增长的设置的工具
title_en: Facebook introduces Accounts Center, a tool for managing the growing number of settings across Facebook, Instagram, and Messenger
